Name: Clefable
Effects: Return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it in your hand.
Flavor-Text: With its acute hearing, it can pick up sounds from far away. It usually hides in quiet places.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy attached to Deoxys and remove 6 damage counters from Deoxys.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.

Name: Espeon
Effects: Move up to 4 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its enemy’s actions.

Name: Forretress
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Does 30 damage times the number of Pineco and Forretress you have in play. This attack does 30 damage to each of your Pineco and Forretress in play.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent’s deck. If tails, discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Hitmontop by attacks is increased by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [R] Energy attached to Ho-Oh.
Flavor-Text: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world’s skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: If your opponent has any [F] Pokémon in play, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage.
Your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Jirachi
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Jirachi from your hand onto your Bench, you may flip 3 coins. For each heads, search your discard pile for a [P] Energy card and attach it to Jirachi.
Effects: Choose a number of your opponent’s Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Jirachi. Remove the highest Stage Evolution card from each of those Pokémon and put those cards back into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Conditions affecting the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

Name: Lucario
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each of your Pokémon in the Lost Zone.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras. Even foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards from your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Magmortar.
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.

Name: Ninetales
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards. This power can’t be used if Ninetales is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Its nine beautiful tails are filled with a wondrous energy that could keep it alive for 1,000 years.

Name: Pachirisu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Pachirisu from your hand onto your Bench, you may attach up to 2 [L] Energy cards from your hand to Pachirisu.
Effects: Put all Energy cards attached to Pachirisu in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: It’s one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

Name: Rayquaza
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy and a [L] Energy attached to Rayquaza.
Flavor-Text: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

Name: Smeargle
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Smeargle is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent’s hand. If you do, choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can’t be used if Smeargle is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Once it becomes an adult, it has a tendency to let its comrades plant footprints on its back.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Umbreon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon than has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Ampharos does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The tail’s tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Cleffa
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Cleffa is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Cleffa by attacks.
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 6 cards. Cleffa is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Granbull
Effects: Granbull does 20 damage to itself. Switch Granbull with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.
Flavor-Text: Because its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.

Name: Meganium
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Remove 2 damage counters from Meganium.
Flavor-Text: Meganium’s breath has the power to revive dead grass and plants. It can make them healthy again.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Look at your opponent’s hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe. It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Mr. Mime
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have both you and your opponent reveal your hands. This power can’t be used if Mr. Mime is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its fingertips emit a peculiar force field that hardens air to create an actual wall.

Name: Pidgeot
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the attack cost of each of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks is [C][C] more.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spreads its beautiful wings wide to frighten its enemies. It can fly at Mach 2 speed.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Search your deck for a [M] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

Name: Slowking
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of either player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order. This power can’t be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: Remove all damage counters from Snorlax. Snorlax can’t use Layabout during your next turn.
Put 1 Energy card attached to Snorlax in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. It can even eat things off the ground.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Tangrowth.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, put 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.

Name: Typhlosion
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Typhlosion.
Flavor-Text: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

Name: Tyrogue
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Tyrogue is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Tyrogue by attacks.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. Tyrogue is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Even though it is small, it can’t be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailing finds all food buried deep underground.

Name: Weezing
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Weezing does 90 damage to itself, and don’t apply Weakness to this damage.
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Zangoose
Effects: During your next turn, Zangoose’s Lost Claw attack’s base damage is 60.
Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and put it in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Its fur would all stand on end if it smelled a Seviper nearby. Its sharp claws tear up its foes.

Name: Bayleef
Flavor-Text: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Donphan
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won’t grow.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Flareon.
Flavor-Text: It stores some of the air it inhales in its internal flame pouch, which heats it to over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Jolteon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Magby
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Magby is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Magby by attacks.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned. Magby is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is found in volcanic craters. Its body temperature is over 1,100 degrees Fahrenheit, so don’t underestimate it.

Name: Mime Jr.
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Mime Jr. is Asleep, prevent all damage done to Mime Jr. by attacks.
Effects: Put the top card of your opponent’s deck in the Lost Zone. Mime Jr. is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy’s movements. Then it wastes no time in making itself scarce!

Name: Pidgeotto
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It renders its prey immobile using well-developed claws, then carries the prey more than 60 miles to its nest.

Name: Quilava
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Quilava.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is fully covered by nonflammable fur. It can withstand any kind of fire attack.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Riolu can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Seviper is now Poisoned.
If Seviper is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage and remove the Special Condition Poisoned from Seviper.
Flavor-Text: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Vaporeon.
Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When Vaporeon’s fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

Name: Chikorita
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from Chikorita.
Chikorita does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sunrays.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn ,any damage done to Clefairy by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

Name: Cyndaquil
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Cyndaquil.
Flavor-Text: It is timid, and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

Name: Hitmonchan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Hitmonchan during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

Name: Hitmonlee
Flavor-Text: If it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.

Name: Houndour
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To corner prey, they check each other’s location using barks that only they can understand.

Name: Koffing
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

Name: Magikarp
Flavor-Text: For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: It dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put it into your hand. If not, discard it and draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Each player draws 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: It loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

Name: Phanpy
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Phanpy has Energy attached to it, any damage done to Phanpy by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As a sign of affection, it bumps with its snout. However, it is so strong, it may send you flying.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle Pidgey and all cards attached to it back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Common in grassy areas and forests, it is very docile and will chase off enemies by flapping up sand.

Name: Pineco
Effects: During your next turn, Pineco’s Surprise Attack attack’s base damage is 80.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Choose a card from your hand and put it in the Lost Zone. Then, draw 3 cards.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Relicanth by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Discovered by chance during deep-sea explorations, it has not changed since ancient times.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.

Name: Snubbull
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn, and any damage done to Teddiursa by attack is reduced by 30 (after applying weakness and resistance).
Flavor-Text: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

Name: Totodile
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to Totodile. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Vulpix.
Flavor-Text: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.

Name: Cheerleader’s Cheer
Description: Draw 3 cards. Your opponent may draw a card.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Dual Ball
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Interviewer’s Questions
Description: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Lost Remover
Description: Put 1 Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

Name: Lost World
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if that player’s opponent has 6 or more Pokémon in the Lost Zone, the player may choose to win the game.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Professor Elm’s Training Method
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Professor Oak’s New Theory
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Research Record
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put as many of them as you like back on top of your deck in any order. Then, put the remaining cards on the bottom of your deck in any order.

Name: Sage’s Training
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose any 2 cards you find there and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this Effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn’t [D]. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Metal Energy
Description: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn’t [M]. Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Deoxys
Effects: Discard 2 [P] Energy attached to Deoxys and remove 6 damage counters from Deoxys.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Dialga
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.

Name: Entei
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Entei’s Retreat Cost is [C] Energy less for each [R] Energy attached to Entei.
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 4 cards of your opponent’s deck. If tails, discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

Name: Ho-Oh
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [R] Energy attached to Ho-Oh.
Flavor-Text: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world’s skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

Name: Raikou
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Raikou’s Retreat Cost is [C] less for each [L] Energy attached to Raikou.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon and don’t apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that races across the land while barking a cry that sounds like crashing thunder.

Name: Rayquaza
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy and a [L] Energy attached to Rayquaza.
Flavor-Text: It flies in the ozone layer, way up high in the sky. Until recently, no one had ever seen it.

Name: Suicune
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Suicune’s Retreat Cost is [C] less for each [W] Energy attached to Suicune.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon races across the land. It is said that north winds will somehow blow whenever it appears.
